statement.en: Promoting Sleep Hygiene in the Intensive Care Unit- Implementation of a Sleep Bundle
statement.summary: Purpose: This quality improvement (QI) project’s purpose was implementation of a sleep bundle for at least 25% of an 18-bed cardiac intensive care unit (ICU) and reduce noise level by 20% (decibels) through education of at least 75% of the patient-care staff 3 months post-implementation. Background: This quality improvement (QI) project’s purpose was implementation of a sleep bundle for at least 25% of an 18-bed cardiac intensive care unit (ICU) and reduce noise level by 20% (decibels) through education of at least 75% of the patient-care staff 3 months post-implementation. Methodology: Patients were assessed using the Richard Campbell Sleep Questionnaire (RSCQ) to evaluate sleep quality pre- and post-bundle. Noise levels were measured via a decibel meter several times and locations, while

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a quality improvement project report (preprint-stage, dated 2026-04-25) describing implementation outcomes. The claims are empirically testable but presented as provisional implementation results pending full peer validation and replication. The methodology is sound but the abstract is incomplete (truncated), making final truth-status assessment premature.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間をかけて騒音を減らし、眠りの質を高めることで、患者の回復が静かに積分される。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 騒音の層が段階的に静寂へ降りていき、患者の目覚めの質が螺旋状に上昇する。
